classes.name: SABA (Rescue)
classes.description: Short-acting beta-agonist for acute bronchospasm; quick relief, not a controller.
classes.plainDescription: The inhaler you grab when you suddenly can't breathe. Works in minutes.

classes.name: ICS (Maintenance)
classes.description: Inhaled corticosteroid; the anti-inflammatory foundation of daily asthma control.
classes.plainDescription: A daily inhaler that calms airway inflammation so attacks happen less.

classes.name: ICS/LABA (Combo)
classes.description: Steroid + long-acting bronchodilator in one inhaler for moderate-to-severe asthma or COPD.
classes.plainDescription: A daily inhaler that combines the steroid with a long-acting opener.

classes.name: LAMA
classes.description: Long-acting muscarinic antagonist. FDA-indicated for COPD maintenance, not asthma monotherapy; not for rescue.
classes.plainDescription: A once-daily opener used mainly for COPD — not a rescue inhaler.

classes.name: Biologics & Non-Inhalers
classes.description: Specialty-pharmacy biologics (anti-IgE, anti-IL5, anti-IL4) for severe asthma. Separate PA pathway; not inhalers.
classes.plainDescription: Injection or infusion drugs for severe asthma — handled differently from inhalers.

records.verificationNote: Read directly from the Maryland Medicaid Pharmacy Program Preferred Drug List, Effective Date 7/1/2026, page 29 of 33, section 'RESPIRATORY' > 'Bronchodilators, Beta Agonists'. The Preferred column lists albuterol HFA (with ProAir HFA/Proventil HFA/Ventolin HFA cited as brand references only, ql notation), albuterol nebulizer solutions (0.083%/5mg/mL and the AccuNeb 0.63mg/1.25mg strengths), and albuterol syrup. Note that this same PDL row also lists 'Serevent' (salmeterol, a LABA) as preferred and arformoterol/formoterol/levalbuterol as non-preferred within the same class row -- those LABA items are out of scope for this SABA class entry and are not carried into the alternatives/paRequired lists here. Non-Preferred / Requires Prior Authorization column (verbatim) lists albuterol tablets, albuterol ER (Vospire ER), levalbuterol nebulizer (Xopenex), levalbuterol HFA (Xopenex HFA, ql), terbutaline (Brethine), ProAir Digihaler, and ProAir RespiClick (ql). No BOGL (brand-over-generic) entry exists for this class in the companion 'Brand Preferred over Generics List' effective 7/1/2026 (daw6-7-1-2026.pdf) -- SABA/albuterol is not listed there. Tier statement is inferred (partial) since the PDL states PA/QL status per drug but does not label cost-sharing tiers explicitly; this is standard convention for Maryland Medicaid FFS (flat copay tiers apply per state Medicaid rules, not stated in this document).

Non-Preferred / Requires Prior Authorization column (verbatim, ICS-only items) lists fluticasone diskus (Flovent Diskus), Pulmicort Flexhaler (ql), and non-Respules Pulmicort, and Alvesco. ICS/LABA combination items (Symbicort, Advair, Dulera, Breo Ellipta, Trelegy Ellipta, AirDuo, etc.) from this same row are captured separately under the icslaba class file, not duplicated here. No BOGL entry applies to plain ICS monotherapy (fluticasone/budesonide/mometasone/beclomethasone) in the companion Brand-over-Generic list effective 7/1/2026. Tier statement is inferred (partial) since the PDL states PA/QL status per drug but not an explicit cost-sharing tier label.

records.verificationNote: Fetched member.carefirst.com/carefirst-resources/pdf/exchange-formulary-sum7277-2026.pdf directly via curl (a plain WebFetch call returned only unparsed binary PDF stream data, consistent with the project's known PDF-extraction limitation) and extracted text locally with pypdf across all 235 pages. Page 1 confirms 'CareFirst Exchange Formulary 2026 ... Last updated 06/01/26' and the plan scope (individual/family, small-group under 51 employees, student health plans). Page 160 (document page 160, labeled '158' in-document), under RESPIRATORY > BETA AGONISTS, lists 'albuterol sulfate inhal aero 108 mcg/act (90mcg base equiv) Tier 1 QL (2 inhalers every 30 days)' as the sole covered HFA rescue inhaler in the class -- no restriction beyond the quantity limit, confirming it as the clean-covered preferred agent. Confirmed by case-insensitive full-document search that ProAir, Ventolin, Proventil, and Xopenex (brand names) do not appear anywhere in the 235-page text extraction, meaning these brands are excluded from the formulary entirely in favor of their generic equivalents (this formulary is generic-first across nearly every class reviewed). Nebulizer solutions, oral albuterol, levalbuterol nebulizer/inhaler forms, and terbutaline are all separately listed Tier 1 with no PA, captured as alternatives. No class-wide step-therapy language appears near this section, and no BOGL (brand-preferred-over-generic) language exists anywhere in the document's legend (page 2) or drug tables.

records.tier: Tier 1 - Generic Drugs ($)
records.verificationNote: Same source as the SABA record: member.carefirst.com/carefirst-resources/pdf/exchange-formulary-sum7277-2026.pdf, fetched via curl and parsed locally with pypdf (plain WebFetch failed to render readable text). Page 1 confirms 'CareFirst Exchange Formulary 2026 ... Last updated 06/01/26'. Page 165 (in-document page numbering), under RESPIRATORY > STEROID INHALANTS, lists: ALVESCO 80/160mcg (Tier 3, QL), ASMANEX HFA 50/100/200mcg (Tier 2, QL), budesonide inhalation suspension 0.25/0.5/1mg/2ml (Tier 1, QL, nebulized), and fluticasone furoate aerosol powder breath-activated 50/100/200mcg (Tier 1, QL) -- all read verbatim from the Drug Name/Drug Tier/Requirements columns. Fluticasone furoate was selected as the preferred agent because it is the only Tier 1 (lowest-cost) dry-powder-inhaler-form ICS monotherapy option suitable for standard adult/adolescent maintenance dosing (budesonide nebulizer suspension is typically reserved for young children who cannot use a handheld inhaler). Confirmed by full-document case-insensitive search that Flovent, QVAR, and Pulmicort brand names do not appear anywhere in the 235-page text, and no beclomethasone product of any kind appears -- consistent with this formulary's pattern of excluding discontinued/superseded brands in favor of generic and covered-brand alternatives. No class-wide step-therapy or BOGL language found near this section.

records.verificationNote: Same source: member.carefirst.com/carefirst-resources/pdf/exchange-formulary-sum7277-2026.pdf, fetched via curl and parsed locally with pypdf. Page 1 confirms 'CareFirst Exchange Formulary 2026 ... Last updated 06/01/26'. Pages 165-166 (in-document numbering), under RESPIRATORY > STEROID/BETA-AGONIST COMBINATIONS, list verbatim: AIRSUPRA 90-80mcg (Tier 2, QL), BREO ELLIPTA 50-25/100-25/200-25mcg (Tier 2, QL), 'breyna' (Tier 1, QL -- an FDA-approved authorized generic name for budesonide-formoterol, listed lowercase per the document's own generic-vs-brand typographic convention explained on page 2), budesonide-formoterol fumarate dihydrate 80-4.5 and 160-4.5 mcg/act (Tier 1, QL), and fluticasone-salmeterol aerosol powder breath-activated 100-50/250-50/500-50 mcg/act (Tier 1, QL). Selected budesonide-formoterol 160-4.5 (higher strength, twice-daily dosing) as the preferred agent since it is a Tier 1 generic covering the most common combination-inhaler use case (moderate-severe asthma/COPD maintenance). Confirmed by full-document case-insensitive search that Symbicort, Advair, Wixela, AirDuo, and Dulera brand names do not appear anywhere in the 235-page text -- Dulera's absence is notable since no mometasone-formoterol combination product (branded or generic) exists in this formulary at all, making it a hard non-formulary exclusion rather than a generic substitution. records.verificationNote: Same source: member.carefirst.com/carefirst-resources/pdf/exchange-formulary-sum7277-2026.pdf, fetched via curl and parsed locally with pypdf. Page 1 confirms 'CareFirst Exchange Formulary 2026 ... Last updated 06/01/26'. Page 159-160 (in-document numbering), under RESPIRATORY > ANTICHOLINERGICS, lists verbatim: ipratropium bromide inhalation solution 0.02% (Tier 1, QL), SPIRIVA RESPIMAT 1.25mcg/2.5mcg (Tier 2, QL), and tiotropium bromide inhalation capsule 18mcg base equivalent (Tier 1, QL) -- no PA or step-therapy code appears on any of these lines. The combination LAMA products (ANTICHOLINERGIC/BETA AGONIST COMBINATIONS on page 158: BEVESPI, ipratropium-albuterol nebulizer, STIOLTO; and ANTICHOLINERGIC/BETA AGONIST/STEROID COMBINATIONS: BREZTRI, TRELEGY) are all Tier 2 brand with QL only, no PA. Selected tiotropium bromide 18mcg capsule as the preferred agent since it is the sole Tier 1 (generic, lowest-cost) LAMA monotherapy option and is FDA-indicated for COPD maintenance (not asthma monotherapy, consistent with the class definition). Confirmed by full-document case-insensitive search that Anoro Ellipta, Incruse Ellipta (umeclidinium), and Tudorza (aclidinium) do not appear anywhere in the document and are tagged nonformulary above -- no generic exists for umeclidinium or aclidinium in the US market as of this review, so their complete absence means CareFirst does not cover those specific molecules in any form (this is a real coverage gap, not simply a brand-vs-generic substitution pattern seen elsewhere in the document). No class-wide step-therapy or BOGL language found near this section.
